_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to participate in democratic decision-making processes like building consensus and voting.

_source.w: We are learning to participate in democratic decision-making processes like building consensus and voting.
y: Practising democratic processes in the classroom builds skills we will use as citizens throughout our lives.
Consensus-building teaches us to listen, negotiate, and find common ground — skills essential for any democracy.
Experiencing democratic decision-making firsthand helps us understand its value and its challenges.
eg: Using consensus-building strategies to make a class decision about a shared issue.
Holding a structured debate where all perspectives are represented before a vote.
Discussing the difference between majority rule and consensus and when each is appropriate.
Running a mock parliament or council meeting with formal procedures.
Reflecting on how the democratic process felt and what made it effective or challenging.
Evaluating whether the outcome of a democratic process was fair to all participants.
